businessSummary.oneLiner: Undisputed AI computing leader — dominating global AI infrastructure through data center GPUs and accelerators
businessSummary.description: NVIDIA is the world's largest semiconductor design company, with AI training and inference data center accelerators as its core revenue driver. FY2026 revenue $215.9B (+65.5% YoY), net income $120.1B (+64.8%). Thursday close $183.14 (-1.55%). Fell with the broader market selloff (S&P -1.52%, Nasdaq -1.78%) as surging oil and Iran tensions hammered equities. GTC 2026 (3/16-19) is 4 days away — 'The Rubin Era approaches as a litmus test for the AI economy.' Market cap $4.45T, P/E 37.38x, Forward P/E 22.20x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Data Center: AI training/inference GPUs (H100, H200, Blackwell B200/GB200/GB300) — ~91% of revenue
Gaming: GeForce RTX GPUs — ~6% of revenue
Professional Visualization: Omniverse, RTX — ~2% of revenue

growthDrivers.description: Thursday's decline pushed Forward P/E to 22.2x. With +65.5% growth, PEG <0.35 — deepening into historically undervalued territory.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: CUDA ecosystem with 15M+ developer lock-in, 90%+ market share in AI training/inference GPUs. Integrated software+hardware platform is the core moat.

moats.description: 15M+ developers locked in. Most AI frameworks optimized for CUDA.
Blackwell→Rubin→Feynman architecture evolution on 2-year cadence. 1-2 generations ahead of competitors.
Chips + systems (DGX) + software (CUDA, AI Enterprise, NemoClaw) + networking (Spectrum-X) + cloud (Nebius investment).

buyReasons.title: Market Selloff Creates Additional Discount — Macro ≠ Fundamentals
buyReasons.rationale: Oil/Iran risks unrelated to NVIDIA's business. Sentiment-driven discount creates buying opportunity.

overallOpinion: NVIDIA closed Thursday at $183.14 (-1.55%), falling with the broader market as the S&P 500 hit its 2026 closing low amid surging oil prices and escalating Iran tensions.

With GTC 2026 (3/16-19) just 4 days away, the market views the 'Rubin Era' as a litmus test for the entire AI economy. Forward P/E at 22.2x against +65.5% growth represents historically compressed valuation — the oil selloff has created an additional discount.

Trading just below the 200-day SMA ($183.70). A successful GTC could catalyze a recapture of both 50-day and 200-day moving averages for a strong technical rebound. Failure would test $175 support. 35/38 analysts maintain Strong Buy with average target $263.29 (+44%). Time to judge on fundamentals, not macro noise.
